person.name: Konrad Rzentarzewski
person.bio: I was there when the config burned. In the age before observability, chaos reigned. The servers howled, and nothing scaled but entropy. Then kube cattle were born — simple, nameless beasts born of children’s games. But they multiplied. And their hunger grew vast. Years passed. The sun revolved the Earth. Then the Earth revolved the Cloud. I grew tired of debugging in darkness. I longed for light. And logs. Preferably structured. That’s when I began preaching — not loudly, just… declaratively. One apply at a time. I walked among DevOps tribes, where bash begat shell, and shell begat pain. To them I brought ConfigMaps, and Secrets hidden in plain sight. Then came the mysterious YAML — a language too holy to lint — inscribed deep within the API’s own heart. Through toil and merge, the infrastructure became manifest. Pods floated on the waters. Deployments begat replicas. Helm bore Charts. Git begat Ops. What was once dev spun to prod. And every service had a name — and every name resolved. Unless external DNS was involved. And the Preachers said: \"Behold, our logs streameth clean.\" Thus began the age of the declarative. We once dwelled in darkness. Now, we scale planetary in full light.

I am passionate about Kubernetes and full lifecycle management of production-grade infrastructures, open standards and currently serving in the advisory board of Linux Foundation Europe.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm thrilled to continue supporting the CNCF, both as a community of like-minded individuals and as a technological ecosystem. My mission is to encourage organizations to adopt open source projects as a foundation for the evolution of their infrastructure and governance, promoting the use of OSS and Open Standards.

person.name: Mauricio Salatino
person.bio: I am an Open Source Software Engineer at @Diagrid, contributing to and driving initiatives for the Dapr OSS project. I also serve as a Steering Committee member for the Knative Project, and I am Co-Leading the Knative Functions initiative. I've been writing on my blog, salaboy.com for over 15 years and I just published a book titled Platform Engineering on Kubernetes for Manning. I previously worked at VMware and Red Hat, building tools to help developers be more productive.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I've been working on Cloud Native and CNCF communities for over seven years now, and I've been constantly trying to give back and connect people who share the same interests around open source projects. Being an ambassador allows me to expand my network to keep connecting, mentoring, and guiding community members to grow.

person.name: Michael Van Slingerland

I use CNCF projects—Cilium, Calico, Istio, Linkerd, Flux, K3s, Harbor, OPA, Trivy, Falco, Kyverno, Fluentd, Jaeger and Prometheus—to build hardened, automated environments. I spend as much time teaching customers how to adopt and refine those solutions, especially around automation and CI/CD pipelines. My goal is simple: ship reliable, high‑quality infrastructure that actually works in the real world.

person.name: Tjandy

He takes a practical, hands-on approach, preferring to delve into technologies directly to understand how they work. Tjandy consistently explores new concepts and technologies, believing that staying current through continuous learning is essential in today's fast-changing tech landscape. His work centers on building reliable, effective systems that solve real problems and make a positive difference for users.

person.name: Rajoul Abdeljalil

person.name: Firas Ben Nacib

person.name: Stefan Lievers

person.name: Thabelo Ramabulana

person.name: Ahmad Al-Masry

person.name: Cathy Zhang
person.bio:
Cathy is a Principal Engineer at Confluent. She has over 20 years of software design
and development experience in a wide range of areas such as serverless, large scale distributed
VM/container orchestration and scheduling, edge computing, container runtime, service mesh,
software defined network, and high availability etc.
Cathy has been an active contributor in the cloud-native computing ecosystems including
Kubernetes, OpenStack, OPNFV, and has led the development of several open-source projects
under CNCF, Linux Foundation Networking, Linux Foundation Edge and Open Infrastructure.
She has made strong contributions to the technical direction and design, driving working group
discussion, and resolving technical disagreements. She co-authored the CNCF serverless
whitepaper, the CloudEvents specification, and the OpenStack service function chaining design.
She has served as the program committee member/track chair for several Kubecons and
OpenStack summits. She has delivered talks at various open-source conferences (Kubecon,
OpenStack Summit, Open Source Summit, OPNFV).

person.name: Zdeněk Váňa

It can help them to solve their issues instead of reinventing the wheel over and over again.
person.company: Prusa Research a.s. / Freelance Cloud Consultant

person.name: Ranjeet Singh

person.name: Benjamin Muschko

I will continue to produce hands-on learning resources in the form of written, and interactive media.

Piotr has shared his knowledge at numerous industry conferences, including a presentation at Kubecon + CloudNativeCon Edge Day in 2023.  He is passionate about Kubernetes and its open-source ecosystem.

person.name: Sébastien Davoult

I love to share and transmit on the subjects which impassion me, I do it with students, conferences, and also by publishing specialized blogs posts.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am very happy and excited to have joined this program. It will allow me to promote the Cloud Native more widely in France. It's a subject that is still unknown in some companies and the specialized content is often not translated from English. My goal is to promote the power of the Cloud Native through French articles, Meetups, and conferences.

person.name: Tharanath G Prabhu

My expertise lies in orchestrating multi-cloud environments, ensuring high availability and disaster recovery across platforms. I prioritize security and compliance, integrating best practices into every stage of the infrastructure lifecycle. I specialize in AWS and Azure platforms, focusing on automating deployments and scaling infrastructure to meet the demands of high-traffic environments. My approach is centered on infrastructure as code, continuous integration, and continuous deployment (CI/CD), ensuring that we can iterate quickly and efficiently while maintaining the highest standards of reliability and security

In addition to my project management duties, I am committed to regularly training my colleagues on Kubernetes, Linux, Prometheus, cloud-native ecosystems, and observability practices, ensuring that our team remains at the cutting edge of industry developments.  As a cloud technology enthusiast, I am constantly seeking ways to improve my skills and deepen my knowledge in these technologies, as well as other rapidly evolving fields.

person.name: 홍한석(Hansuk Hong)

Now, I’m committed to using that journey to teach, motivate, and inspire young people. Through my non-profit organization, The Gift INC, I want to show them that they too can build successful careers in IT.
